pi_items.source: Code analysis: verifier.py line 62 checks 'if min_circ >= 0.84' but metadata and description both claim 0.85. Error message on line 66 references 0.85 but actual check is 0.84.
Code analysis: verifier.py line 73 checks 'if max_circ <= 1.01' with tolerance for rounding. Metadata claims 1.0, verifier allows 1.01.
Code analysis: verifier.py line 87 checks 'if 10 <= csv_count <= 60'. Matches metadata claim.

Code analysis: verifier.py line 87 checks 'if 10 <= csv_count <= 60'. Verifier comment at line 82 states 'Blobs image has ~60-70 blobs' suggesting actual image may have more, but metadata bound of 60 is what verifier enforces.

Code analysis: setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results directory (lines 8-9). export_result.sh looks for circular_rois.zip at this path (lines 12, 24). Task description specifies ~/ImageJ_Data/results/circular_rois.zip which expands to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/circular_rois.zip for user ga.

Code analysis: setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results directory (lines 8-9). export_result.sh looks for filtered_measurements.csv at this path (lines 13, 25). Task description specifies ~/ImageJ_Data/results/filtered_measurements.csv.

pi_items.verified_value: Blobs (25K) is a real built-in sample image in Fiji
pi_items.source: Web search and code analysis: setup_task.sh references 'Blobs (25K)' sample accessible via File > Open Samples in Fiji (line 64). ImageJ documentation confirms 'Blobs (25K)' is an official built-in sample image used for demonstrations.

Code and documentation analysis: The task uses the built-in 'Blobs (25K)' sample image, which is a synthetic demo image provided with Fiji for testing and demonstration purposes, not real biological data.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the built-in Blobs (25K) synthetic sample image in Fiji. The output files are ROI manager exports at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/circular_rois.zip and measurements CSV at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/filtered_measurements.csv. The expected ROI count range is 10-60. A critical discrepancy exists: the metadata and task description claim a circularity minimum threshold of 0.85, but the verifier code actually checks for >= 0.84 (more lenient). Maximum circularity is verified as 1.0 (with 1.01 tolerance for rounding).
